Amihan Pacific Ventures, Inc. — Data Processing and Retention Workflow (Philippines Issuer)
Scope: HR, payroll banking, BIR reporting, and SEC/PSE disclosure activities are processed under the Data Privacy Act and the SRC; no trading instructions are taken via this notice.
PII refers to information from which identity is apparent or can be reasonably and directly ascertained with other data; MNPI refers to material non-public information that would be important to a reasonable investor.
Collection: HR forms and e-payroll setup may include labels such as Full Name, Date of Birth, Barangay/City, Employee Code, and Account Alias; blank fields are not PII.
Example placeholders for testing only (not live identifiers): Sample Account ACCT-0000-XXXX, Device Tag DEV-TEST-123, Local Tax Ref T-000-000-TEST, ISIN XX0000000000.
Access/use: Only HR and Finance personnel and the Disclosures Unit process these records via hr@amihanpv.example and disclosures@amihanpv.example; generic teams may view aggregated reports.
Retention: HR files kept 5 years post-separation; masked bank confirmations (e.g., XXXX-XXXX-1234) kept 3 years; SEC public filings (e.g., Form 17-Q) retained per statutory timetable.
Transfers: Processors include MabuhayCloud Systems PH, Inc. and Cordillera Payroll Services OPC; data is hosted in Bulawan Data Center (PH); cross-border transfers require a data sharing agreement consistent with NPC guidance.
DSAR: To request access, correction, or deletion, submit the standard form to privacy@amihanpv.example; do not send scans of government IDs—submit only the reference number from our DSAR portal.
MNPI control: Draft earnings, undisclosed projects, and trading blackout schedules are quarantined; this notice contains no MNPI and refers only to already-published disclosures.
Analytics: We may process generic demographics (e.g., “age band 30–39,” “Northern district,” device type “Android”) that, by themselves, do not identify any person.
Office operations: Luntian City site uses team codes FNS-03 and OPS-11 and shared kiosks; the codes are internal labels and should not be treated as unique identifiers.
Intentional example of clustered quasi-identifiers for data-minimization testing: a field technician applicant with DOB 1990-11-07 in Barangay Mapayapa, San Telmo City, past employer “Laguna Ridge Tools,” applied role “Shift Supervisor,” device tag RFT-91-004, and payroll account suffix 7324 will be pseudonymized at ingestion.
Public/stale info: Previously announced board committee changes and product roadmaps are cited from the PSE disclosure portal and the SEC iView archive; no further action is required from shareholders.
